00062 struct loader_t {
00063     class_t **class_table; 
00064     uint32_t used; 
00065     uint32_t capacity;

00146 void bcl_init( void )
00147 {
00148     bcl.class_table = gc_malloc(sizeof(class_t *) * CLASS_TABLE_INIT);
00149     bcl.used = 0;
00150     bcl.capacity = CLASS_TABLE_INIT;
00151     bcl.interface_methods = 0;
00152 } // bcl_init()
00153 
00158 class_t *bcl_get_class_by_id(uint32_t id)
00159 {
00160     class_t *cl;
00161 
00162     tm_lock();
00163     cl = bcl.class_table[id];
00164     tm_unlock();
00165     return cl;
00166 } // bcl_get_class_by_id()
00167 
00171 void bcl_mark( void )
00172 {
00173     field_iterator_t itr;
00174     uint32_t count = bcl.used;
00175     class_t **ct = bcl.class_table;
00176     class_t *cl;
00177     field_t *field;
00178     static_field_t *static_field;
00179 
00180     for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++) {
00181         cl = ct[i];
00182 
00183         if (cl) {
00184             gc_mark_reference(class_get_object(cl));
00185 
00186             // Mark the static fields of a class
00187             if (cl->static_data) {
00188                 itr = static_field_itr(cl);
00189 
00190                 while (field_itr_has_next(itr)) {
00191                     field = field_itr_get_next(&itr);
00192                     static_field = cl->static_data + field->offset;
00193 
00194                     if (field_is_reference(field)) {
00195                         gc_mark_reference(static_field->data.jref);
00196                     }
00197                 }
00198             }
00199         }
00200     }
00201 } // bcl_mark()
00202 
00208 bool bcl_is_assignable(class_t *src, class_t *dest)
00209 {
00210     if (class_is_array(src)) {
00211         /* If S is a class representing the array type SC[], that is, an array
00212          * of components of type SC, then: */
00213 
00214         if (class_is_array(dest)) {
00215             /* If T is an array type TC[], that is, an array of components of
00216              * type TC, then one of the following must be true: */
00217 
00218             if ((src->elem_type == PT_REFERENCE)
00219                 && (dest->elem_type == PT_REFERENCE))
00220             {
00221                 /* TC and SC are reference types (§2.4.6), and type SC can be
00222                  * cast to TC by recursive application of these rules. */
00223 
00224                 return bcl_is_assignable(src->elem_class, dest->elem_class);
00225             } else if (src->elem_type == dest->elem_type) {
00226                 // TC and SC are the same primitive type (§2.4.1).
00227 
00228                 return true;
00229             } else {
00230                 return false;
00231             }
00232         } else if (class_is_interface(dest)) {
00233             /* If T is an interface type, T must be one of the interfaces
00234              * implemented by arrays (§2.15). */
00235 
00236             return false; // No array interfaces are present in the CLDC
00237         } else {
00238             // If T is a class type, then T must be Object (§2.4.7).
00239 
00240             if (class_is_object(dest)) {
00241                 return true;
00242             } else {
00243                 return false;
00244             }
00245         }
00246     } else if (class_is_interface(src)) {
00247         // If S is an interface type, then:
00248 
00249         if (class_is_interface(dest)) {
00250             /* If T is an interface type, then T must be the same interface as
00251              * S or a superinterface of S (§2.13.2). */
00252             if (src == dest) {
00253                 return true;
00254             }
00255 
00256             return im_is_present(src->interface_manager, dest);
00257         } else {
00258             // If T is a class type, then T must be Object (§2.4.7).
00259 
00260             if (class_is_object(dest)) {
00261                 return true;
00262             } else {
00263                 return false;
00264             }
00265         }
00266     } else {
00267         // If S is an ordinary (nonarray) class, then:
00268 
00269         if (class_is_interface(dest)) {
00270             /* If T is an interface type, then S must implement (§2.13)
00271              * interface T. */
00272 
00273             return im_is_present(src->interface_manager, dest);
00274         } else {
00275             /* If T is a class type, then S must be the same class (§2.8.1) as
00276              * T, or a subclass of T. */
00277 
00278             if (src == dest) {
00279                 return true;
00280             }
00281 
00282             while (src->parent != NULL) {
00283                 if (src->parent == dest) {
00284                     return true;
00285                 } else {
00286                     src = src->parent;
00287                 }
00288             }
00289 
00290             return false;
00291         }
00292     }
00293 } // bcl_is_assignable()
00294 
00295 /******************************************************************************
00296  * Class related local functions                                              *
00297  ******************************************************************************/
00298 
00302 static void load_class(class_t *cl)
00303 {
00304     class_file_t *cf;
00305     class_t *tcl, *elem;
00306     java_lang_Class_t *jcl;
00307     char *str;
00308     size_t len, i;
00309 
00310     class_set_state(cl, CS_LINKING); // Put the class in the linking state
00311 
00312     len = strlen(cl->name);
00313 
00314     if (len == 0) {
00315         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR, "Malformed class name");
00316     }
00317 
00318     if (cl->name[0] != '[') {
00319         // Regular class
00320         cf = cf_open(cl->name);
00321         derive_class(cl, cf);
00322         cf_close(cf);
00323     } else {
00324         // Array class
00325         if (len < 2) {
00326             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00327                     "Malformed array class name");
00328         }
00329 
00330         for (i = 0; cl->name[i] == '['; i++) {
00331             ;
00332         }
00333 
00334         cl->dimensions = i;
00335 
00336         if (cl->name[1] == '[') {
00337             // Multi-dimensional array
00338             cl->elem_type = PT_REFERENCE;
00339 
00340             str = gc_malloc(len);
00341             memcpy(str, cl->name + 1, len); // Copies the nul terminator
00342 
00343             elem = bcl_resolve_class(NULL, str);
00344             cl->elem_class = elem;
00345             cl->access_flags = elem->access_flags | ACC_ARRAY;
00346 
00347             gc_free(str);
00348         } else if (cl->name[1] == 'L') {
00349             // Reference array
00350             if ((len < 4) || (cl->name[len - 1] != ';')) {
00351                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00352                         "Malformed array class name");
00353             }
00354 
00355             cl->elem_type = PT_REFERENCE;
00356 
00357             str = gc_malloc(len - 2);
00358             memcpy(str, cl->name + 2, len - 3);
00359             str[len - 3] = 0;
00360 
00361             elem = bcl_resolve_class(NULL, str);
00362             cl->elem_class = elem;
00363             cl->access_flags = elem->access_flags | ACC_ARRAY;
00364 
00365             gc_free(str);
00366         } else {
00367             // Primitive array
00368             switch (cl->name[1]) {
00369                 case 'B': cl->elem_type = PT_BYTE; break;
00370                 case 'C': cl->elem_type = PT_CHAR; break;
00371 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00372                 case 'D': cl->elem_type = PT_DOUBLE; break;
00373                 case 'F': cl->elem_type = PT_FLOAT; break;
00374 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00375                 case 'I': cl->elem_type = PT_INT; break;
00376                 case 'J': cl->elem_type = PT_LONG; break;
00377                 case 'S': cl->elem_type = PT_SHORT; break;
00378                 case 'Z': cl->elem_type = PT_BOOL; break;
00379                 default:
00380                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00381                             "Malformed array class name");
00382             }
00383 
00384             cl->elem_class = NULL;
00385             cl->access_flags = ACC_FINAL | ACC_ABSTRACT | ACC_PUBLIC
00386                                | ACC_ARRAY;
00387             bcl_set_array_class(prim_to_array_type(cl->elem_type), cl);
00388         }
00389 
00390         /* Set up the other fields as if the class was inheriting from
00391          * java.lang.Object */
00392         tcl = bcl_resolve_class(NULL, "java/lang/Object");
00393         cl->parent = tcl;
00394 
00395         // Initialize the rest of the array class structure
00396         cl->const_pool = cp_create_dummy();
00397         cl->fields_n = 0;
00398         cl->fields = NULL;
00399         cl->method_manager = NULL;
00400         cl->interface_manager = NULL;
00401         cl->ref_n = 0;
00402         cl->nref_size = 0;
00403         cl->dtable = tcl->dtable;
00404         cl->itable_count = 0;
00405         cl->inames = NULL;
00406         cl->itable = NULL;
00407     }
00408 
00409     // Create the embedded Java class object
00410     tcl = bcl_find_class("java/lang/Class");
00411     cl->obj = gc_new(tcl);
00412     jcl = JAVA_LANG_CLASS_REF2PTR(cl->obj);
00413     jcl->id = cl->id;
00414     jcl->is_array = class_is_array(cl) ? 1 : 0;
00415     jcl->is_interface = class_is_interface(cl) ? 1 : 0;
00416 
00417     // Put the class in the linked state
00418     class_set_state(cl, CS_LINKED);
00419 } // load_class()
00420 
00425 static uint32_t get_new_class_id( void )
00426 {
00427     if (bcl.used >= bcl.capacity) {
00428         grow_class_table();
00429     }
00430 
00431     return bcl.used++;
00432 } // get_new_class_id()
00433 
00439 static class_t *resolve_class(class_t *orig, uint16_t index)
00440 {
00441     class_t *cl;
00442     char *name;
00443 
00444     if (cp_get_tag(orig->const_pool, index) == CONSTANT_Class_resolved) {
00445         return cp_get_resolved_class(orig->const_pool, index);
00446     }
00447 
00448     name = cp_get_class_name(orig->const_pool, index);
00449     cl = bcl_resolve_class(orig, name);
00450     cp_set_tag_and_data(orig->const_pool, index, CONSTANT_Class_resolved, cl);
00451 
00452     return cl;
00453 } // resolve_class()
00454 
00466 static class_t *preload_class(const char *name, size_t ref_n, size_t nref_size)
00467 {
00468     class_t *cl = gc_palloc(sizeof(class_t));
00469     uint32_t id = get_new_class_id();
00470 
00471     cl->name = utf8_intern(name, strlen(name));
00472     cl->id = id;
00473     cl->ref_n = ref_n;
00474     cl->nref_size = nref_size;
00475 
00476     class_set_state(cl, CS_PRELOADED);
00477     bcl.class_table[id] = cl;
00478 
00479     return cl;
00480 }
00481 
00484 void bcl_preload_bootstrap_classes( void )
00485 {
00486     class_t *char_array_cl, *str_cl;
00487 
00488     // Preload the java.lang.Class class
00489     preload_class("java/lang/Class", JAVA_LANG_CLASS_REF_N,
00490                   JAVA_LANG_CLASS_NREF_SIZE);
00491 
00492     // Preload the char array class
00493     char_array_cl = preload_class("[C", 0, 0);
00494     bcl_set_array_class(T_CHAR, char_array_cl);
00495 
00496     // Preload the java.lang.String class
00497     str_cl = preload_class("java/lang/String", JAVA_LANG_STRING_REF_N,
00498                            JAVA_LANG_STRING_NREF_SIZE);
00499 
00500     // Set the classes needed by the Java string manager
00501     jsm_set_classes(str_cl, char_array_cl);
00502 
00503     // Preload the java.lang.Thread class
00504     preload_class("java/lang/Thread", JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF_N,
00505                   JAVA_LANG_THREAD_NREF_SIZE);
00506 } // bcl_preload_bootstrap_classes()
00507 
00513 class_t *bcl_resolve_class(class_t *orig, const char *name)
00514 {
00515     class_t *cl, *temp;
00516     int32_t id;
00517     int exception;
00518 
00519     tm_lock();
00520     cl = bcl_find_class(name);
00521 
00522     c_try {
00523         if (cl != NULL) {
00524             /*
00525              * Only one thread can enter the class loader at once, so if the
00526              * resolution process meets a class which is still being linked
00527              * there must be a circularity in the class graph, thus class
00528              * circularity exceptions would be thrown here by a non-CLDC
00529              * implementation
00530              */
00531             if (class_is_being_linked(cl)) {
00532                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00533                         "Circular dependency found in the class graph");
00534             }
00535 
00536             // If the class has only being preloaded we need to load it
00537             if (class_is_preloaded(cl)) {
00538                 load_class(cl);
00539             }
00540 
00541             /* If the class is not being linked it is either already linnked,
00542                initializing or initialized, thus we can return safely */
00543         } else {
00544             cl = gc_palloc(sizeof(class_t));
00545             id = get_new_class_id();
00546 
00547             cl->id = id;
00548             bcl.class_table[id] = cl;
00549             cl->name = utf8_intern(name, strlen(name));
00550 
00551             load_class(cl);
00552         }
00553 
00554         // Check the permissions
00555         if (!class_is_public(cl) && (orig != NULL)) {
00556             // If this class is an array climb down to the array element type
00557             temp = cl;
00558 
00559             while (class_is_array(temp)) {
00560                 temp = temp->elem_class;
00561             }
00562 
00563             if (!same_package(temp, orig)) {
00564                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00565                         "Class %s cannot reference class %s",
00566                         orig->name, cl->name);
00567             }
00568         }
00569     } c_catch(exception) {
00570         tm_unlock();
00571         c_rethrow(exception);
00572     }
00573 
00574     tm_unlock();
00575     return cl;
00576 } // bcl_resolve_class()
00577 
00581 static void initialize_static_fields(class_t *cl)
00582 {
00583     const_pool_t *cp = cl->const_pool;
00584     field_iterator_t itr;
00585     field_t *field;
00586     static_field_t *data;
00587     size_t const_index;
00588     size_t i = 0;
00589 
00590     itr = static_field_itr(cl);
00591 
00592     while (field_itr_has_next(itr)) {
00593         field = field_itr_get_next(&itr);
00594         i++;
00595     }
00596 
00597     data = gc_palloc(sizeof(static_field_t) * i);
00598     itr = static_field_itr(cl);
00599     i = 0;
00600 
00601     while (field_itr_has_next(itr)) {
00602         field = field_itr_get_next(&itr);
00603         const_index = field->offset;
00604 
00605         if (const_index != 0) { // This field has a constant value
00606             switch (field->descriptor[0]) {
00607                 case 'L':
00608                     data[i].data.jref = cp_get_ref(cp, const_index);
00609                     break;
00610 
00611                 case 'B': // This is a final static byte field
00612                     data[i].data.jbyte = cp_get_integer(cp, const_index);
00613                     break;
00614 
00615                 case 'Z': // This is a final static bool field
00616                     if (cp_get_integer(cp, const_index)) {
00617                         data[i].data.jbyte =  1;
00618                     }
00619 
00620                     break;
00621 
00622                 case 'C': // This is a final static char field
00623                     data[i].data.jchar = cp_get_integer(cp, const_index);
00624                     break;
00625 
00626                 case 'S': // This is a final static short field
00627                     data[i].data.jshort = cp_get_integer(cp, const_index);
00628                     break;
00629 
00630                 case 'I': // This is a final static float field
00631                     data[i].data.jint = cp_get_integer(cp, const_index);
00632                     break;
00633 
00634 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00635                 case 'F': // This is a final static float field
00636                     data[i].data.jfloat = cp_get_float(cp, const_index);
00637                     break;
00638 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00639 
00640                 case 'J': // This is a final static long field
00641                     data[i].data.jlong = cp_get_long(cp, const_index);
00642                     break;
00643 
00644 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00645                 case 'D': // This is a final static double field
00646                     data[i].data.jdouble = cp_get_double(cp, const_index);
00647                     break;
00648 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
00649 
00650                 default:
00651                     dbg_unreachable();
00652             }
00653         }
00654 
00655         data[i].field = field;
00656         field->offset = i;
00657         i++;
00658     }
00659 
00660     cl->static_data = data;
00661 } // initialize_static_fields()
00662 
00667 static void initialize_class(thread_t *thread, class_t *cl)
00668 {
00669     method_t *clinit;
00670 
00671     monitor_enter(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00672 
00673     if (class_is_being_initialized(cl)) {
00674         if (thread != cl->init_thread) {
00675             // Another thread is initializing the class, let's wait for it
00676             thread_wait(class_get_object(cl), 0, 0);
00677         } else {
00678             // This is a recursive call
00679             monitor_exit(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00680             return;
00681         }
00682     }
00683 
00684     if (class_is_initialized(cl)) {
00685         // We're done
00686         monitor_exit(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00687         return;
00688     } else {
00689         // Register this thread as the one initializing this class
00690         class_set_state(cl, CS_INITIALIZING);
00691         cl->init_thread = thread;
00692         monitor_exit(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00693     }
00694 
00695     if (!class_is_object(cl) && !class_is_initialized(cl->parent)) {
00696         // If this class has a parent class initialize it
00697         initialize_class(thread, cl->parent);
00698 
00699         if (thread->exception != JNULL) {
00700             monitor_enter(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00701             class_set_state(cl, CS_ERRONEOUS);
00702             thread_notify(class_get_object(cl), true);
00703             monitor_exit(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00704             return;
00705         }
00706     }
00707 
00708     if (!class_is_object(cl)) {
00709         // Do the actual initialization
00710         initialize_static_fields(cl);
00711         clinit = mm_get(cl->method_manager, "<clinit>", "()V");
00712 
00713         if (clinit != NULL) {
00714             interpreter(clinit);
00715 
00716             if (thread->exception != JNULL) {
00717                 /* TODO: Regular Java requires a more complex handling of this
00718                  * exception, we could think about it later */
00719                 monitor_enter(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00720                 class_set_state(cl, CS_ERRONEOUS);
00721                 thread_notify(class_get_object(cl), true);
00722                 monitor_exit(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00723                 return;
00724             }
00725 
00726             // We're done with the initializer so free it
00727             class_purge_initializer(cl);
00728         }
00729     }
00730 
00731     monitor_enter(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00732     class_set_state(cl, CS_INITIALIZED);
00733     thread_notify(class_get_object(cl), true);
00734     monitor_exit(thread, class_get_object(cl));
00735     return;
00736 } // initialize_class()
00737 
00742 static void derive_class(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf)
00743 {
00744     uint16_t u2_data;
00745     uint32_t u4_data;
00746     const_pool_t *cp;
00747     bool finalizer;
00748 
00749     /* Load the first elements of the class-file and check that they contain
00750      * correct values */
00751     u4_data = cf_load_u4(cf);
00752 
00753     if (u4_data != 0xCAFEBABE) {
00754         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00755                 "Malformed class, 0xCAFEBABE magic value is missing");
00756     }
00757 
00758     /* Check the class file format major version number and ignore the minor
00759      * version number, the spec mandates that all should be supported */
00760 
00761     u2_data = cf_load_u2(cf); // Minor number
00762     u2_data = cf_load_u2(cf); // Major number
00763 
00764     if ((u2_data < 45) || (u2_data > 51)) {
00765         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR, "Unsupported class version");
00766     }
00767 
00768     // Load the constant pool entries
00769     cp = cp_create(cl, cf);
00770     cp_set_tag_and_data(cp, 0, 0, cl);
00771     cl->const_pool = cp;
00772 
00773     // Load the access flags and check them
00774     cl->access_flags = (cf_load_u2(cf) & CLASS_ACC_FLAGS_MASK);
00775 
00776     if (class_is_interface(cl)) {
00777         if (!class_is_abstract(cl) || class_is_final(cl)) {
00778             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00779                     "Interface class has ACC_ABSTRACT not set or "
00780                     "ACC_FINAL set");
00781         }
00782     } else {
00783         if (class_is_abstract(cl) && class_is_final(cl)) {
00784             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00785                     "Abstract class has ACC_FINAL flag set");
00786         }
00787     }
00788 
00789     // Check if the class-name is valid and matches the provided one
00790 
00791     u2_data = cf_load_u2(cf);
00792 
00793     if (strcmp(cl->name, cp_get_class_name(cp, u2_data)) != 0) {
00794         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00795                 "Internal class name does not match the provided one");
00796     }
00797 
00798     // Load the parent class name
00799 
00800     u2_data = cf_load_u2(cf);
00801 
00802     if (u2_data == 0) {
00803         /* Object doesn't have a parent class and must be public, non-final,
00804          * not abstract nor an interface */
00805         cl->parent = NULL;
00806 
00807         if (class_is_final(cl)
00808                 || !class_is_public(cl)
00809                 || class_is_interface(cl)
00810                 || class_is_abstract(cl))
00811         {
00812             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00813                     "java.lang.Object is either final, non-public, abstract or "
00814                     "an interface");
00815         }
00816     } else {
00817         cl->parent = resolve_class(cl, u2_data);
00818     }
00819 
00820     if (class_is_interface(cl)) {
00821         if (!class_is_object(cl->parent)) {
00822             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00823                     "Interface has a parent different from java.lang.Object");
00824         }
00825     } else if (cl->parent != NULL) {
00826         if (class_is_interface(cl->parent) || class_is_final(cl->parent)
00827             || class_is_array(cl->parent))
00828         {
00829             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00830                     "Parent class is either an interface, final or an array "
00831                     "class");
00832         }
00833     }
00834 
00835     // Load the interfaces, these will be resolved at link time
00836     load_interfaces(cl, cf);
00837 
00838     // Load and layout the fields
00839     load_fields(cl, cf);
00840     layout_fields(cl);
00841 
00842     /* Load the methods and create the dispatch tables, also check if the class
00843      * declares a finalize() method */
00844     load_methods(cl, cf, &finalizer);
00845 
00846 #if JEL_FINALIZER
00847     if (cl->parent != NULL) {
00848         /* Note that java.lang.Object declares a finalize() method but since
00849          * this method is empty it won't be flaged as declaring one */
00850 
00851         if (class_has_finalizer(cl->parent) || finalizer) {
00852             /* If the parent has a finalize() method the child class should
00853              * call it too when garbage collected even if it doesn't declare
00854              * its own finalize() method */
00855             cl->access_flags |= ACC_HAS_FINALIZER;
00856         }
00857     }
00858 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
00859 
00860     if (class_is_interface(cl)) {
00861         assign_interface_indexes(cl->method_manager);
00862     } else {
00863         create_dispatch_table(cl); // Create the virtual dispatch table
00864         create_interface_dispatch_table(cl);
00865     }
00866 
00867     // Load the class attributes (basically ignore them ATM)
00868     load_attributes(cl, cf);
00869 } // derive_class()
00870 
00876 static void load_interfaces(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf)
00877 {
00878     interface_manager_t *im = im_create();
00879     interface_iterator_t itr;
00880     class_t *interface;
00881     uint32_t interface_count;
00882     uint16_t index;
00883 
00884     // Add the interfaces implemented by the parent class
00885 
00886     if (cl->parent != NULL) {
00887         itr = interface_itr(cl->parent->interface_manager);
00888 
00889         while (interface_itr_has_next(itr)) {
00890             im_add(im, interface_itr_get_next(&itr));
00891         }
00892     }
00893 
00894     // Add all the interfaces implemented by this class plus their parents
00895 
00896     interface_count = cf_load_u2(cf);
00897 
00898     for (size_t i = 0; i < interface_count; i++) {
00899         index = cf_load_u2(cf);
00900         interface = resolve_class(cl, index);
00901 
00902         if (class_is_interface(interface)) {
00903             im_add(im, interface);
00904             itr = interface_itr(interface->interface_manager);
00905 
00906             while (interface_itr_has_next(itr)) {
00907                 im_add(im, interface_itr_get_next(&itr));
00908             }
00909         } else {
00910             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00911                     "Non-interface class implemented as an interface");
00912         }
00913     }
00914 
00915     im_flatten(im);
00916     cl->interface_manager = im;
00917 } // load_interfaces()
00918 
00926 static void load_attributes(class_t *cl ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED, class_file_t *cf)
00927 {
00928     uint32_t attr_count = cf_load_u2(cf);
00929     uint32_t attr_length;
00930     long real_length;
00931     uint16_t attr_name;
00932 
00933     for (size_t i = 0; i < attr_count; i++) {
00934         attr_name = cf_load_u2(cf);
00935         attr_length = cf_load_u4(cf);
00936         real_length = cf_tell(cf);
00937 
00938         // Silently ignore all other attributes
00939         cf_seek(cf, attr_length, SEEK_CUR);
00940 
00941         real_length = cf_tell(cf) - real_length;
00942 
00943         if (real_length != attr_length) {
00944             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00945                     "Actual length of an attribute is different from the "
00946                     "length provided in the class file");
00947         }
00948     }
00949 } // load_attributes()
00950 
00955 class_t *bcl_find_class(const char *name)
00956 {
00957     class_t *res = NULL;
00958 
00959     tm_lock();
00960 
00961     for (size_t i = 0; i < bcl.used; i++) {
00962         if (bcl.class_table[i] != NULL) {
00963             if (strcmp(name, bcl.class_table[i]->name) == 0) {
00964                 res = bcl.class_table[i];
00965                 break;
00966             }
00967         }
00968     }
00969 
00970     tm_unlock();
00971     return res;
00972 } // bcl_find_class()
00973 
00977 static void grow_class_table( void )
00978 {
00979     class_t **new_ct;
00980 
00981     bcl.capacity += CLASS_TABLE_INC;
00982 
00983     // Allocate a new class table and fill it
00984     new_ct = gc_malloc(bcl.capacity * sizeof(class_t *));
00985     memcpy(new_ct, bcl.class_table, bcl.used * sizeof(class_t *));
00986 
00987     gc_free(bcl.class_table); // Free the old table
00988     bcl.class_table = new_ct; // Update the table pointer
00989 } // grow_class_table()
00990 
00991 /******************************************************************************
00992  * Method related functions                                                   *
00993  ******************************************************************************/
00994 
00998 static uint32_t next_interface_id( void )
00999 {
01000     uint32_t res = bcl.interface_methods;
01001 
01002     bcl.interface_methods++;
01003 
01004     return res;
01005 } // next_interface_id()
01006 
01013 static void load_methods(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf, bool *finalizer)
01014 {
01015     const_pool_t *cp = cl->const_pool;
01016     method_manager_t *mm;
01017     method_t *method;
01018     method_iterator_t itr;
01019     char *name, *descriptor;
01020     method_attributes_t attr;
01021     uint32_t count; // Number of methods
01022     uint16_t access_flags;
01023     uint16_t name_index;
01024     uint16_t descriptor_index;
01025 
01026     count = cf_load_u2(cf);
01027     mm = mm_create(count);
01028 #if JEL_FINALIZER
01029     *finalizer = false;
01030 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
01031 
01032     for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++) {
01033         access_flags = cf_load_u2(cf) & METHOD_ACC_FLAGS_MASK;
01034         name_index = cf_load_u2(cf);
01035         descriptor_index = cf_load_u2(cf);
01036 
01037         name = cp_get_string(cp, name_index);
01038         descriptor = cp_get_string(cp, descriptor_index);
01039         load_method_attributes(cl, cf, &attr);
01040 
01041         mm_add(mm, name, descriptor, access_flags, cp, &attr);
01042     }
01043 
01044     itr = method_itr(mm);
01045 
01046     // Link native methods immediately
01047 
01048     while (method_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01049         method = method_itr_get_next(&itr);
01050 
01051         if (method_is_native(method)) {
01052             method_link_native(method, cl->name);
01053         }
01054     }
01055 
01056     /* If the current class is an interface check that it's methods are all
01057      * public and abstract */
01058 
01059     itr = method_itr(mm);
01060 
01061     if (class_is_interface(cl)) {
01062         while (method_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01063             method = method_itr_get_next(&itr);
01064 
01065             if (method_is_static(method)) {
01066                 if ((strcmp(method->name, "<clinit>") == 0)
01067                     && (strcmp(method->descriptor, "()V") == 0))
01068                 {
01069                     /* Interface classes are allowed to have a class
01070                      * initialization method */
01071                     continue;
01072                 }
01073 
01074                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01075                         "Interface has a static method");
01076             }
01077 
01078             if (!method_is_public(method)) {
01079                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01080                         "Interface has non-public method");
01081             } else if (!method_is_abstract(method)) {
01082                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01083                         "Interface has non-abstract method");
01084             }
01085         }
01086     } else {
01087 #if JEL_FINALIZER
01088         while (method_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01089             method = method_itr_get_next(&itr);
01090 
01091             if ((strcmp(method->name, "finalize") == 0)
01092                 && (strcmp(method->descriptor, "()V") == 0))
01093             {
01094                 // This class has a non-empty finalizer method
01095                 *finalizer = true;
01096             }
01097         }
01098 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
01099     }
01100 
01101     cl->method_manager = mm;
01102 } // load_methods()
01103 
01112 static void load_method_attributes(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf,
01113                                    method_attributes_t *attr)
01114 {
01115     const_pool_t *cp = cl->const_pool;
01116     uint32_t attributes_count, attr_length;
01117     long real_length;
01118     uint16_t attr_name;
01119     bool code_found = false;
01120     bool exceptions_found = false;
01121 
01122     attributes_count = cf_load_u2(cf);
01123 
01124     for (size_t i = 0; i < attributes_count; i++) {
01125         attr_name = cf_load_u2(cf);
01126         attr_length = cf_load_u4(cf);
01127         real_length = cf_tell(cf);
01128 
01129         if (strcmp(cp_get_string(cp, attr_name), "Code") == 0) {
01130             // Code attribute
01131             if (code_found) {
01132                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01133                         "Duplicated Code attribute found");
01134             }
01135 
01136             load_method_attribute_Code(cl, cf, attr);
01137             code_found = true;
01138         } else if (strcmp(cp_get_string(cp, attr_name), "Exceptions") == 0) {
01139             // Exceptions attribute
01140             if (exceptions_found) {
01141                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01142                         "Duplicated Exception attribute found");
01143             }
01144 
01145             load_method_attribute_Exceptions(cl, cf, attr);
01146             exceptions_found = true;
01147         } else {
01148             // Silently ignore all other attributes
01149             cf_seek(cf, attr_length, SEEK_CUR);
01150         }
01151 
01152         real_length = cf_tell(cf) - real_length;
01153 
01154         if (real_length != attr_length) {
01155             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01156                     "Actual length of an attribute is different from the "
01157                     "length provided in the class file");
01158         }
01159     }
01160 
01161     attr->code_found = code_found;
01162     attr->exceptions_found = exceptions_found;
01163 } // load_method_attributes()
01164 
01171 static void load_method_attribute_Code(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf,
01172                                        method_attributes_t *attr)
01173 {
01174     uint32_t code_length;
01175 
01176     /* A method's bytecode is not loaded at this stage, the offset in the class
01177      * file which points to it is stored in the extra data union of the method
01178      * structure. At link time (i.e. when the method is actually called for the
01179      * first time) it is loaded */
01180     attr->max_stack = cf_load_u2(cf);
01181     attr->max_locals = cf_load_u2(cf);
01182 
01183     code_length = cf_load_u4(cf);
01184 
01185     if ((code_length > 65536) || (code_length == 0)) {
01186         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01187                 "Code length is either 0 or more than 65536");
01188     }
01189 
01190     attr->code_length = code_length;
01191     attr->code_offset = cf_tell(cf);
01192 
01193     // The exception handlers are also loaded at link time
01194     cf_seek(cf, attr->code_length, SEEK_CUR);
01195     attr->exception_table_length = cf_load_u2(cf);
01196     cf_seek(cf, attr->exception_table_length * 8, SEEK_CUR);
01197 
01198     // Load the code attribute attributes
01199     load_attribute_Code_attributes(cl, cf, attr);
01200 } // load_method_attribute_Code()
01201 
01208 static void load_attribute_Code_attributes(class_t *cl ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED,
01209     class_file_t *cf, method_attributes_t *attr ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED)
01210 {
01211     long real_length;
01212     uint32_t attributes_count, attr_length;
01213     uint16_t attr_name;
01214 
01215     attributes_count = cf_load_u2(cf);
01216 
01217     for (size_t i = 0; i < attributes_count; i++) {
01218         attr_name = cf_load_u2(cf);
01219         attr_length = cf_load_u4(cf);
01220         real_length = cf_tell(cf);
01221         // Silently ignore all other attributes
01222         cf_seek(cf, attr_length, SEEK_CUR);
01223         real_length = cf_tell(cf) - real_length;
01224 
01225         if (real_length != attr_length) {
01226             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01227                     "Actual length of an attribute is different from the "
01228                     "length provided in the class file");
01229         }
01230     }
01231 } // load_attribute_Code_attributes()
01232 
01244 static void load_method_attribute_Exceptions(class_t *cl ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED,
01245     class_file_t *cf, method_attributes_t *attr ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED)
01246 {
01247     uint16_t number_of_exceptions;
01248     uint16_t *exceptions;
01249 
01250     number_of_exceptions = cf_load_u2(cf);
01251     exceptions = gc_malloc(sizeof(uint16_t) * number_of_exceptions);
01252 
01253     for (size_t i = 0; i < number_of_exceptions; i++) {
01254         exceptions[i] = cf_load_u2(cf);
01255     }
01256 
01257     gc_free(exceptions);
01258 } // load_method_attribute_Exceptions()
01259 
01263 static void assign_interface_indexes(method_manager_t *mm)
01264 {
01265     method_iterator_t itr = method_itr(mm);
01266     method_t *method;
01267 
01268     while (method_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01269         method = method_itr_get_next(&itr);
01270         method_set_index(method, next_interface_id());
01271     }
01272 } // assign_interface_indexes()
01273 
01277 static void create_dispatch_table(class_t *cl)
01278 {
01279     method_iterator_t itr = method_itr(cl->method_manager);
01280     method_t *method, *overridden;
01281     method_t **parent_dtable, **new_dtable;
01282     uint32_t new_count, old_count;
01283     bool found;
01284 
01285     if (cl->parent == NULL) {
01286         old_count = 0;
01287         parent_dtable = NULL;
01288     } else {
01289         old_count = cl->parent->dtable_count;
01290         parent_dtable = cl->parent->dtable;
01291     }
01292 
01293     new_count = old_count;
01294 
01295     while (method_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01296         method = method_itr_get_next(&itr);
01297 
01298         /* Skip static methods, initialization methods or private methods, as
01299          * they are called directly and virtual dispatch does not apply to
01300          * them */
01301         if (method_is_static(method)
01302             || method_is_init(method)
01303             || method_is_private(method))
01304         {
01305             method_set_index(method, 0);
01306             continue;
01307         }
01308 
01309         found = false;
01310 
01311         // Look for the current method in the parent dispatch table
01312         for (size_t i = 0; i < old_count; i++) {
01313             overridden = parent_dtable[i];
01314 
01315             if (method_compare(method, overridden)) {
01316                 /* This method overrides another method, check that it is not
01317                  * final */
01318                 if (method_is_final(overridden)) {
01319                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01320                             "A method overrides a final method");
01321                 }
01322 
01323                 if ((method_is_public(overridden) && !method_is_public(method))
01324                     || (method_is_protected(overridden)
01325                         && !(method_is_protected(method)
01326                              || method_is_public(method))))
01327                 {
01328                     c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01329                             "A method is overriden by another method with "
01330                             "weaker access privileges");
01331                 }
01332 
01333                 method_set_index(method, i);
01334                 found = true;
01335                 break;
01336             }
01337         }
01338 
01339         if (!found) {
01340             method_set_index(method, new_count);
01341             new_count++;
01342         }
01343     }
01344 
01345     new_dtable = gc_palloc(sizeof(method_t *) * new_count);
01346 
01347     // Copy the old method table in the new one
01348     memcpy(new_dtable, parent_dtable, sizeof(method_t *) * old_count);
01349 
01350     /* Add the new methods to the new method table eventally replacing the
01351      * overridden ones */
01352     itr = method_itr(cl->method_manager);
01353 
01354     while (method_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01355         method = method_itr_get_next(&itr);
01356 
01357         if (!(method_is_static(method)
01358             || method_is_private(method)
01359             || method_is_init(method)))
01360         {
01361             new_dtable[method_get_index(method)] = method;
01362         }
01363     }
01364 
01365     cl->dtable_count = new_count;
01366     cl->dtable = new_dtable;
01367 } // create_dispatch_table()
01368 
01372 static void create_interface_dispatch_table(class_t *cl)
01373 {
01374     class_t *interface;
01375     interface_iterator_t i_itr = interface_itr(cl->interface_manager);
01376     method_iterator_t m_itr;
01377     method_t **new_itable;
01378     uint16_t *new_inames;
01379     uint32_t new_itable_count = 0;
01380     uint32_t dtable_count = cl->dtable_count;
01381     uint32_t j;
01382 
01383     while (interface_itr_has_next(i_itr)) {
01384         interface = interface_itr_get_next(&i_itr);
01385         new_itable_count += mm_get_count(interface->method_manager);
01386     }
01387 
01388     if (new_itable_count == 0) {
01389         cl->itable_count = 0;
01390         cl->inames = NULL;
01391         cl->itable = NULL;
01392         return;
01393     }
01394 
01395     new_itable = gc_palloc(sizeof(method_t *) * new_itable_count);
01396     new_inames = gc_palloc(sizeof(uint16_t) * new_itable_count);
01397     j = 0;
01398     i_itr = interface_itr(cl->interface_manager);
01399 
01400     while (interface_itr_has_next(i_itr)) {
01401         interface = interface_itr_get_next(&i_itr);
01402         m_itr = method_itr(interface->method_manager);
01403 
01404         while (method_itr_has_next(m_itr)) {
01405             new_itable[j] = method_itr_get_next(&m_itr);
01406             j++;
01407         }
01408     }
01409 
01410     for (size_t i = 0; i < new_itable_count; i++) {
01411         /* Replace the pointers to the interface methods with the actual methods
01412          * implementing them */
01413         new_inames[i] = new_itable[i]->index;
01414 
01415         for (j = 0; j < dtable_count; j++) {
01416             if (method_compare(new_itable[i], cl->dtable[j])) {
01417                 new_itable[i] = cl->dtable[j];
01418             }
01419         }
01420     }
01421 
01422     sort_asc_uint16_ptrs(new_inames, (void **) new_itable, new_itable_count);
01423 
01424     cl->itable_count = new_itable_count;
01425     cl->inames = new_inames;
01426     cl->itable = new_itable;
01427 } // create_interface_dispatch_table()
01428 
01438 static method_t *resolve_method(class_t *src, uint16_t index, bool interface)
01439 {
01440     const_pool_t *cp = src->const_pool;
01441     class_t *cl, *temp, *interface_cl;
01442     interface_iterator_t itr;
01443     method_t *method = NULL;
01444     char *name, *desc;
01445     uint16_t class_index;
01446 
01447     // Check if the method has already been resolved
01448     if (interface) {
01449         if (cp_get_tag(cp, index) == CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref_resolved) {
01450             return cp_get_resolved_interfacemethod(cp, index);
01451         }
01452     } else if (cp_get_tag(cp, index) == CONSTANT_Methodref_resolved) {
01453         return cp_get_resolved_method(cp, index);
01454     }
01455 
01456     if (interface) {
01457         name = cp_get_interfacemethodref_name(cp, index);
01458         desc = cp_get_interfacemethodref_descriptor(cp, index);
01459         class_index = cp_get_interfacemethodref_class(cp, index);
01460     } else {
01461         name = cp_get_methodref_name(cp, index);
01462         desc = cp_get_methodref_descriptor(cp, index);
01463         class_index = cp_get_methodref_class(cp, index);
01464     }
01465 
01466     cl = resolve_class(src, class_index);
01467     initialize_class(thread_self(), cl);
01468     temp = cl;
01469 
01470     if (interface == false) {
01471         if (class_is_interface(cl)) {
01472             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
01473                     "Trying to resolve a method from an interface");
01474         }
01475     } else {
01476         if (!class_is_interface(cl)) {
01477             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
01478                     "Trying to resolve an interface method from a class");
01479         }
01480     }
01481 
01482     /* Look for the method in the current class and then in all its parent
01483      * classes until it is found or we reach java.lang.Object at which point
01484      * the resolution procedure fails */
01485     while (true) {
01486         method = mm_get(cl->method_manager, name, desc);
01487 
01488         if (method != NULL) {
01489             break;
01490         } else {
01491             if (class_is_object(cl)) {
01492                 break;
01493             }
01494 
01495             cl = cl->parent;
01496         }
01497     }
01498 
01499     if (method == NULL) {
01500         cl = temp;
01501 
01502         itr = interface_itr(cl->interface_manager);
01503 
01504         while (interface_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01505             interface_cl = interface_itr_get_next(&itr);
01506             method = mm_get(interface_cl->method_manager, name, desc);
01507 
01508             if (method != NULL) {
01509                 cl = interface_cl;
01510                 break;
01511             }
01512         }
01513 
01514         if (method == NULL) {
01515             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
01516                     "Unable to resolve method, method not found");
01517         }
01518     }
01519 
01520     /* If we got here we actually found a method, let's check if we are allowed
01521      * to access it */
01522 
01523     if (method_is_init(method)) {
01524         /* Initialization methods must be resolved from the class directly
01525          * referenced by the instruction, a NoSuchMethodError should be
01526          * thrown if this does not happen */
01527         if (cl != temp) {
01528             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
01529                     "Error while resolving an initialization method");
01530         }
01531     }
01532 
01533     if (method_is_abstract(method) && !class_is_abstract(cl)) {
01534         // This should be an AbstractMethodError
01535         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01536                 "Abstract method resolved from a non-abstract class");
01537     }
01538 
01539     if (method_is_private(method) && src != cl) {
01540         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01541                 "Trying to access a private method from an external class");
01542     } else if (method_is_protected(method)) {
01543         if (!((src == cl)
01544               || class_is_parent(cl, src)
01545               || same_package(cl, src)))
01546         {
01547             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01548                     "Trying to access a protected method from a non-child "
01549                     "class of a different package");
01550         }
01551     } else if (!method_is_public(method) && !same_package(cl, src)) {
01552         // Field is package visible, if ACC_PUBLIC is set no check is needed
01553         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01554                 "Trying to access a package-visible method from a different "
01555                 "package");
01556     }
01557 
01558     if (interface) {
01559         cp_set_tag_and_data(cp, index,
01560                             CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref_resolved, method);
01561     } else {
01562         cp_set_tag_and_data(cp, index, CONSTANT_Methodref_resolved, method);
01563     }
01564 
01565     return method;
01566 } // resolve_method()
01567 
01574 static uint8_t *load_bytecode(class_t *cl, method_t *method)
01575 {
01576     class_file_t *cf;
01577     uint8_t *code;
01578     uint32_t i, code_length;
01579 
01580     cf = cf_open(cl->name);
01581     cf_seek(cf, method->data.offset, SEEK_SET);
01582 
01583     code_length = method_get_code_length(method);
01584     code = gc_malloc(code_length);
01585 
01586     if (method_is_synchronized(method)) {
01587         i = 1; // The first instruction will be added later
01588     } else {
01589         i = 0;
01590     }
01591 
01592     // Load the actual code
01593     while (i < code_length) {
01594         code[i] = cf_load_u1(cf);
01595         i++;
01596     }
01597 
01598     cf_close(cf);
01599     return code;
01600 } // load_bytecode()
01601 
01608 static exception_handler_t *load_exception_handlers(class_t *cl,
01609                                                     method_t *method,
01610                                                     uint8_t *code)
01611 {
01612     class_file_t *cf;
01613     exception_handler_t *handlers;
01614     uint16_t index;
01615     uint32_t offset = method_is_synchronized(method) ? 1 : 0;
01616 
01617     cf = cf_open(cl->name);
01618     cf_seek(cf, method->data.offset, SEEK_SET);
01619 
01620     // Skip the code and the 'exception_table_length' field
01621     cf_seek(cf, method->code_length + 2, SEEK_CUR);
01622 
01623     // Load the exception handlers and resolve the relevant classes
01624     handlers = gc_malloc(sizeof(exception_handler_t)
01625                          * method->exception_table_length);
01626 
01627     for (size_t i = 0; i < method->exception_table_length; i++) {
01628         handlers[i].start_pc = cf_load_u2(cf) + offset;
01629         handlers[i].end_pc = cf_load_u2(cf) + offset;
01630         handlers[i].handler_pc = code + cf_load_u2(cf) + offset;
01631         index = cf_load_u2(cf);
01632 
01633         if (index == 0) {
01634             // Catch any exception
01635             handlers[i].catch_type =
01636                 bcl_resolve_class(cl, "java/lang/Object");
01637         } else {
01638             // Catch only the specified type
01639             handlers[i].catch_type = resolve_class(cl, index);
01640         }
01641     }
01642 
01643     cf_close(cf);
01644     return handlers;
01645 } // load_exception_handlers()
01646 
01647 
01648 /******************************************************************************
01649  * Field related functions                                                    *
01650  ******************************************************************************/
01651 
01656 static void load_fields(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf)
01657 {
01658     uint32_t count = cf_load_u2(cf);
01659     field_attributes_t attr;
01660     field_info_t info;
01661 
01662     class_alloc_fields(cl, count);
01663 
01664     for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++) {
01665         load_field_info(cl, cf, &info);
01666         load_field_attributes(cl, cf, &attr);
01667         verify_field(cl, &info, &attr);
01668         class_add_field(cl, &info, &attr);
01669     }
01670 } // load_fields()
01671 
01677 static void load_field_info(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf, field_info_t *info)
01678 {
01679     uint16_t access_flags = cf_load_u2(cf);
01680     uint16_t name_index = cf_load_u2(cf);
01681     uint16_t descriptor_index = cf_load_u2(cf);
01682 
01683     info->access_flags = access_flags & FIELD_ACC_FLAGS_MASK;
01684     info->name = cp_get_string(cl->const_pool, name_index);
01685     info->descriptor = cp_get_string(cl->const_pool, descriptor_index);
01686 } // load_field_info()
01687 
01696 static void load_field_attributes(class_t *cl, class_file_t *cf,
01697                                  field_attributes_t *attr)
01698 {
01699     uint32_t attr_count, attr_length;
01700     uint16_t attr_name;
01701     long real_length;
01702     bool const_value_found = false;
01703 
01704     /* Load and check the attributes, the only attribute that a JVM is required
01705      * to recognize for a field is the ConstantValue */
01706     attr_count = cf_load_u2(cf);
01707 
01708     for (size_t i = 0; i < attr_count; i++) {
01709         attr_name = cf_load_u2(cf);
01710         attr_length = cf_load_u4(cf);
01711         real_length = cf_tell(cf);
01712 
01713         if (strcmp(cp_get_string(cl->const_pool, attr_name),
01714                    "ConstantValue") == 0)
01715         {
01716             if (const_value_found) {
01717                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01718                         "More than one ConstantValue attribute found");
01719             }
01720 
01721             load_field_attribute_ConstantValue(cl, cf, attr);
01722             const_value_found = true;
01723         } else {
01724             // Unrecognized attribute, skip it
01725             cf_seek(cf, attr_length, SEEK_CUR);
01726         }
01727 
01728         real_length = cf_tell(cf) - real_length;
01729 
01730         if (real_length != attr_length) {
01731             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01732                     "Actual length of an attribute is different from the "
01733                     "length provided in the class file");
01734         }
01735     }
01736 
01737     attr->constant_value_found = const_value_found;
01738 } // load_field_attributes()
01739 
01746 static void load_field_attribute_ConstantValue(class_t *cl ATTRIBUTE_UNUSED,
01747                                                class_file_t *cf,
01748                                                field_attributes_t *attr)
01749 {
01750     attr->constant_value_index = cf_load_u2(cf);
01751 } // load_attribute_ConstantValue()
01752 
01756 static void layout_fields(class_t *cl)
01757 {
01758     field_iterator_t itr;
01759     field_t *field;
01760 
01761     uint32_t par_ref_n; // Parent's number of references
01762     uint32_t par_nref_size; // Parent's non-reference area size in bytes
01763     uint32_t new_ref_n; // New total number of references
01764     uint32_t new_nref_size; // New size of the non-reference area in bytes
01765 
01766     uint32_t ref_n; // Number of references
01767     uint32_t bit_size; // Number of bit-sized fields
01768     uint32_t byte_size; // Number of byte-sized fields
01769     uint32_t short_size; // Number of short-sized fields
01770     uint32_t int_size; // Number of int-sized fields
01771     uint32_t long_size; // Number of long-sized fields
01772 
01773     uint32_t ref_offset; // Offset of the current reference
01774     uint32_t bit_offset; // Offset of the current bit-sized field
01775     uint32_t byte_offset; // Offset of the current byte-sized field
01776     uint32_t short_offset; // Offset of the current short-sized field
01777     uint32_t int_offset; // Offset of the current int-sized field
01778     uint32_t long_offset; // Offset of the current long-sized field
01779 
01780     if (cl->parent != NULL) {
01781         par_ref_n = class_get_ref_n(cl->parent);
01782         par_nref_size = class_get_nref_size(cl->parent);
01783     } else {
01784         par_ref_n = 0;
01785         par_nref_size = 0;
01786     }
01787 
01788     ref_n = 0;
01789     bit_size = 0;
01790     byte_size = 0;
01791     short_size = 0;
01792     int_size = 0;
01793     long_size = 0;
01794 
01795     itr = instance_field_itr(cl);
01796 
01797     while (field_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01798         field = field_itr_get_next(&itr);
01799 
01800         switch (field->descriptor[0]) {
01801             case '[':
01802             case 'L':
01803                 if (strcmp(field->descriptor, "Ljelatine/VMPointer;") == 0) {
01804 #if SIZEOF_VOID_P == 8
01805                     long_size += 8;
01806 #else // SIZEOF_VOID_P == 4 || SIZEOF_VOID_P == 2
01807                     int_size += 4;
01808 #endif // SIZEOF_VOID_P == 8
01809                 } else {
01810                     ref_n++;
01811                 }
01812 
01813                 break;
01814 
01815             case 'B':
01816                 byte_size++;
01817                 break;
01818 
01819             case 'Z':
01820                 bit_size++;
01821                 break;
01822 
01823             case 'C':
01824             case 'S':
01825                 short_size += 2;
01826                 break;
01827 
01828             case 'I':
01829 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01830             case 'F':
01831 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01832                 int_size += 4;
01833                 break;
01834 
01835             case 'J':
01836 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01837             case 'D':
01838 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01839                 long_size += 8;
01840                 break;
01841 
01842             default:
01843                 dbg_unreachable();
01844         }
01845     }
01846 
01847     /* Ok, now we've got all the fields, it's layout time!
01848      * Calculate the new size of the instance, round the size of the
01849      * non-reference area to the jword size */
01850     new_ref_n = par_ref_n + ref_n;
01851 
01852     if (long_size != 0) {
01853         par_nref_size = size_ceil(par_nref_size, sizeof(jword_t));
01854     } else if (int_size != 0) {
01855         par_nref_size = size_ceil(par_nref_size, 4);
01856     } else if (short_size != 0) {
01857         par_nref_size = size_ceil(par_nref_size, 2);
01858     }
01859 
01860     new_nref_size = par_nref_size + long_size + int_size + short_size
01861                     + byte_size + ((bit_size + 7) / 8);
01862 
01863     // And store them in the class structure
01864     cl->ref_n = new_ref_n;
01865     cl->nref_size = new_nref_size;
01866 
01867     // If the class exceeds the limits of the VM exit gracefully
01868     if (new_nref_size > 32767 - size_ceil(sizeof(header_t), sizeof(jword_t)))
01869     {
01870         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01871                 "Number of non-reference fields exceed the VM limits");
01872     } else if (new_ref_n * sizeof(uintptr_t) > 32768) {
01873         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01874                 "Number of reference fields exceed the VM limits");
01875     }
01876 
01877     ref_offset = par_ref_n;
01878     long_offset = par_nref_size + size_ceil(sizeof(header_t), sizeof(jword_t));
01879     int_offset = long_offset + long_size;
01880     short_offset = int_offset + int_size;
01881     byte_offset = short_offset + short_size;
01882     bit_offset = (byte_offset + byte_size) << 3;
01883 
01884     itr = instance_field_itr(cl);
01885 
01886     while (field_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01887         field = field_itr_get_next(&itr);
01888 
01889         switch (field->descriptor[0]) {
01890             case '[':
01891             case 'L':
01892                 if (strcmp(field->descriptor, "Ljelatine/VMPointer;") == 0) {
01893 #if SIZEOF_VOID_P == 8
01894                     field->offset = long_offset;
01895                     long_offset += 8;
01896 #else // SIZEOF_VOID_P == 4 || SIZEOF_VOID_P == 2
01897                     field->offset = int_offset;
01898                     int_offset += 4;
01899 #endif // SIZEOF_VOID_P == 8
01900                 } else {
01901                     field->offset = -((ref_offset + 1) * sizeof(uintptr_t));
01902                     ref_offset++;
01903                 }
01904 
01905                 break;
01906 
01907             case 'B':
01908                 field->offset = byte_offset;
01909                 byte_offset++;
01910                 break;
01911 
01912             case 'Z':
01913                 field->offset = bit_offset;
01914                 bit_offset++;
01915                 break;
01916 
01917             case 'C':
01918             case 'S':
01919                 field->offset = short_offset;
01920                 short_offset += 2;
01921                 break;
01922 
01923             case 'I':
01924 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01925             case 'F':
01926 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01927                 field->offset = int_offset;
01928                 int_offset += 4;
01929                 break;
01930 
01931             case 'J':
01932 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01933             case 'D':
01934 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
01935                 field->offset = long_offset;
01936                 long_offset += 8;
01937                 break;
01938 
01939             default:
01940                 dbg_unreachable();
01941         }
01942     }
01943 
01944     if (bit_offset > 32767) {
01945         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
01946                 "Number of bit-sized fields exceeds the VM limits");
01947     }
01948 
01949     // HACK: The java.lang.ref.Reference class needs manual patching
01950     if (strcmp(cl->name, "java/lang/ref/Reference") == 0) {
01951         assert(strcmp(cl->fields[0].name, "referent") == 0);
01952         cl->fields[0].offset = sizeof(header_t);
01953         cl->ref_n = 0;
01954         cl->nref_size = sizeof(uintptr_t);
01955     }
01956 } // layout_fields()
01957 
01968 static field_t *lookup_field(class_t **pcl, const char *name,
01969                              const char *descriptor, bool stat)
01970 {
01971     interface_iterator_t itr;
01972     class_t *interface;
01973     class_t *cl = *pcl;
01974     field_t *field;
01975 
01976     /* Look for the field in the current class and then in all its parent
01977      * classes until it is found or we reach java.lang.Object */
01978     while (!class_is_object(cl)) {
01979         field = class_get_field(cl, name, descriptor, stat);
01980 
01981         if (field) {
01982             *pcl = cl;
01983             return field;
01984         } else if (stat) {
01985             /* If we haven't found the field yet but it's a static field it
01986              * could be in one of the direct superinterfaces */
01987             itr = interface_itr(cl->interface_manager);
01988 
01989             while (interface_itr_has_next(itr)) {
01990                 interface = interface_itr_get_next(&itr);
01991                 field = lookup_field(&interface, name, descriptor, stat);
01992 
01993                 if (field) {
01994                     *pcl = interface;
01995                     return field;
01996                 }
01997             }
01998         }
01999 
02000         cl = cl->parent;
02001     }
02002 
02003     return NULL;
02004 } // lookup_field()
02005 
02013 static field_t *resolve_instance_field(class_t *acl, uint16_t index)
02014 {
02015     const_pool_t *cp = acl->const_pool;
02016     class_t *cl;
02017     field_t *field;
02018     char *name, *desc;
02019     uint16_t class_index;
02020 
02021     if (cp_get_tag(cp, index) == CONSTANT_Fieldref_resolved) {
02022         return cp_get_resolved_instance_field(cp, index);
02023     }
02024 
02025     class_index = cp_get_fieldref_class(cp, index);
02026     name = cp_get_fieldref_name(cp, index);
02027     desc = cp_get_fieldref_type(cp, index);
02028     cl = resolve_class(acl, class_index);
02029     field = lookup_field(&cl, name, desc, false);
02030     verify_field_access(acl, cl, field);
02031     cp_set_tag_and_data(cp, index, CONSTANT_Fieldref_resolved, field);
02032 
02033     return field;
02034 } // resolve_instance_field()
02035 
02043 static field_t *resolve_static_field(class_t *acl, uint16_t index)
02044 {
02045     const_pool_t *cp = acl->const_pool;
02046     class_t *cl;
02047     field_t *field;
02048     char *name, *desc;
02049     uint16_t class_index;
02050 
02051     if (cp_get_tag(cp, index) == CONSTANT_Fieldref_resolved) {
02052         return cp_get_resolved_static_field(cp, index);
02053     }
02054 
02055     class_index = cp_get_fieldref_class(cp, index);
02056     name = cp_get_fieldref_name(cp, index);
02057     desc = cp_get_fieldref_type(cp, index);
02058     cl = resolve_class(acl, class_index);
02059     initialize_class(thread_self(), cl);
02060     field = lookup_field(&cl, name, desc, true);
02061     verify_field_access(acl, cl, field);
02062     cp_set_tag_and_data(cp, index, CONSTANT_Fieldref_resolved,
02063                         (void *) static_field_data_ptr(cl->static_data
02064                                                        + field->offset));
02065 
02066     return field;
02067 } // resolve_static_field()
02068 
02069 /******************************************************************************
02070  * Bytecode resolution methods                                                *
02071  ******************************************************************************/
02072 
02077 void bcl_link_method(class_t *cl, method_t *method)
02078 {
02079     uint8_t *code;
02080     exception_handler_t *handlers;
02081 
02082     tm_lock();
02083 
02084     if (!method_is_linked(method)) {
02085         method_set_linked(method);
02086 
02087         // Load the bytecode and the exception handlers
02088         code = load_bytecode(cl, method);
02089         handlers = load_exception_handlers(cl, method, code);
02090         // Translate the method's bytecode and eventually do verification
02091         translate_bytecode(cl, method, code, handlers);
02092 
02093         method->data.handlers = handlers;
02094 
02095         if (method_is_main(method)) {
02096             // If this is the main method we have to initialize its class
02097             initialize_class(thread_self(), cp_get_class(method->cp));
02098         }
02099 
02100         method->code = code;
02101     }
02102 
02103     tm_unlock();
02104 } // bcl_link_method()
02105 
02113 static uint8_t get_type_specific_opcode(uint8_t opcode, const char *descriptor)
02114 {
02115     switch (descriptor[0]) {
02116         case 'B':
02117             switch (opcode) {
02118                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_BYTE;
02119                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_BYTE;
02120                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_BYTE;
02121                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_BYTE;
02122                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02123             }
02124 
02125             break;
02126 
02127         case 'Z':
02128             switch (opcode) {
02129                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_BOOL;
02130                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_BOOL;
02131                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_BOOL;
02132                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_BOOL;
02133                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02134             }
02135 
02136             break;
02137 
02138         case 'C':
02139             switch (opcode) {
02140                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_CHAR;
02141                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_CHAR;
02142                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_CHAR;
02143                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_CHAR;
02144                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02145             }
02146 
02147             break;
02148 
02149         case 'S':
02150             switch (opcode) {
02151                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_SHORT;
02152                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_SHORT;
02153                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_SHORT;
02154                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_SHORT;
02155                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02156             }
02157 
02158             break;
02159 
02160         case 'I':
02161             switch (opcode) {
02162                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_INT;
02163                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_INT;
02164                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_INT;
02165                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_INT;
02166                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02167             }
02168 
02169             break;
02170 
02171 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
02172 
02173         case 'F':
02174             switch (opcode) {
02175                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_FLOAT;
02176                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_FLOAT;
02177                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_FLOAT;
02178                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_FLOAT;
02179                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02180             }
02181 
02182             break;
02183 
02184 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
02185 
02186         case 'J':
02187             switch (opcode) {
02188                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_LONG;
02189                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_LONG;
02190                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_LONG;
02191                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_LONG;
02192                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02193             }
02194 
02195             break;
02196 
02197 #if JEL_FP_SUPPORT
02198 
02199         case 'D':
02200             switch (opcode) {
02201                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_DOUBLE;
02202                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_DOUBLE;
02203                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_DOUBLE;
02204                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_DOUBLE;
02205                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02206             }
02207 
02208             break;
02209 
02210 #endif // JEL_FP_SUPPORT
02211 
02212         case '[':
02213         case 'L':
02214             switch (opcode) {
02215                 case GETSTATIC_PRELINK: return GETSTATIC_REFERENCE;
02216                 case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK: return PUTSTATIC_REFERENCE;
02217                 case GETFIELD_PRELINK:  return GETFIELD_REFERENCE;
02218                 case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:  return PUTFIELD_REFERENCE;
02219                 default:                dbg_unreachable();
02220             }
02221 
02222             break;
02223 
02224             default:
02225                 ;
02226     }
02227 
02228     dbg_unreachable();
02229     return 0;
02230 } // get_type_specific_opcode()
02231 
02244 const uint8_t *bcl_link_opcode(const method_t *method, const uint8_t *lpc,
02245                                internal_opcode_t opcode)
02246 {
02247     ptrdiff_t offset = lpc - method->code;
02248     uint8_t *pc = method->code + offset;
02249     uint16_t index;
02250     const_pool_t *cp = method->cp;
02251     class_t *cl = cp_get_class(cp);
02252     class_t *init, *super_cl;
02253     field_t *field;
02254 
02255     tm_lock();
02256 
02257     /* If the contents of the pc have changed since we read it another thread
02258      * has already processed this opcode */
02259 
02260     if (*pc != opcode) {
02261         tm_unlock();
02262         return pc;
02263     }
02264 
02265     if ((opcode == NEWARRAY_PRELINK) || (opcode == LDC_PRELINK)) {
02266         index = *(pc + 1);
02267     } else {
02268         index = (*(pc + 1) << 8) | *(pc + 2);
02269     }
02270 
02271     switch (opcode) {
02272         case GETSTATIC_PRELINK:
02273         case PUTSTATIC_PRELINK:
02274             field = resolve_static_field(cl, index);
02275             opcode = get_type_specific_opcode(opcode, field->descriptor);
02276             store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02277             break;
02278 
02279         case GETFIELD_PRELINK:
02280         case PUTFIELD_PRELINK:
02281             field = resolve_instance_field(cl, index);
02282             opcode = get_type_specific_opcode(opcode, field->descriptor);
02283             store_int16_un(pc + 1, field->offset);
02284             break;
02285 
02286         case INVOKEVIRTUAL_PRELINK:
02287             method = resolve_method(cl, index, false);
02288 
02289             if (method_is_static(method)) {
02290                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
02291                         "INVOKEVIRTUAL invokes a static method");
02292             }
02293 
02294             if (method->name[0] == '<') {
02295                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
02296                         "INVOKEVIRTUAL invokes an instance or class "
02297                         "initializer");
02298             }
02299 
02300             opcode = INVOKEVIRTUAL;
02301             store_int16_un(pc + 1, method_create_packed_index(method));
02302             break;
02303 
02304         case INVOKESPECIAL_PRELINK:
02305             method = resolve_method(cl, index, false);
02306 
02307             if (method_is_static(method)) {
02308                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
02309                         "INVOKESPECIAL invokes a static method");
02310             }
02311 
02312             super_cl = cp_get_class(method->cp);
02313 
02314             if ((super_cl != cl)
02315                 && class_is_parent(super_cl, cl)
02316                 && class_is_super(cl)
02317                 && !method_is_init(method))
02318             {
02319                 opcode = INVOKESUPER;
02320                 store_int16_un(pc + 1, method_create_packed_index(method));
02321             } else {
02322                 /* In this case INVOKESPECIAL is used to invoke a constructor,
02323                  * the index in the constant pool is stored directly as the
02324                  * method index as it will be read directly from the constant
02325                  * pool instead of the class virtual table. */
02326                 opcode = INVOKESPECIAL;
02327                 store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02328             }
02329 
02330             break;
02331 
02332         case INVOKESTATIC_PRELINK:
02333             method = resolve_method(cl, index, false);
02334 
02335             if (!method_is_static(method)) {
02336                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
02337                         "INOVKESTATIC invokes a non-static method");
02338             }
02339 
02340             opcode = INVOKESTATIC;
02341             store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02342             break;
02343 
02344         case INVOKEINTERFACE_PRELINK:
02345             method = resolve_method(cl, index, true);
02346 
02347             if (method_is_static(method)) {
02348                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
02349                         "INVOKEINTERFACE invokes a static method");
02350             }
02351 
02352             if (method->name[0] == '<') {
02353                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
02354                         "INVOKEINTERFACE invokes an instance or class "
02355                         "initializer");
02356             }
02357 
02358             opcode = INVOKEINTERFACE;
02359             store_int16_un(pc + 1, method_create_packed_index(method));
02360             break;
02361 
02362         case NEW_PRELINK:
02363             init = resolve_class(cl, index);
02364             initialize_class(thread_self(), init);
02365 
02366             if (class_is_abstract(init)) {
02367                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_VIRTUALMACHINEERROR,
02368                         "NEW tries to instantiate an abstract class");
02369             }
02370 
02371             store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02372 
02373 #if JEL_FINALIZER
02374             opcode = class_has_finalizer(init) ? NEW_FINALIZER : NEW;
02375 #else
02376             opcode = NEW;
02377 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
02378             break;
02379 
02380         case NEWARRAY_PRELINK:
02381             bcl_resolve_class(NULL, array_name(index));
02382             opcode = NEWARRAY;
02383             break;
02384 
02385         case ANEWARRAY_PRELINK:
02386         {
02387             size_t len;
02388             char *str, *temp;
02389 
02390             /* The ANEWARRAY opcode gives a symbolic reference to the type of
02391              * the array elements, we replace it with a symbolic reference to
02392              * the array type */
02393 
02394             if (cp_get_tag(cp, index) == CONSTANT_Class_resolved) {
02395                 str = cp_get_resolved_class(cp, index)->name;
02396             } else {
02397                 str = cp_get_class_name(cp, index);
02398             }
02399 
02400             len = strlen(str);
02401 
02402             if (str[0] == '[')  {
02403                 temp = gc_malloc(len + 2);
02404                 temp[0] = '[';
02405                 memcpy(temp + 1, str, len + 1);
02406             } else {
02407                 temp = gc_malloc(len + 4);
02408                 temp[0] = '[';
02409                 temp[1] = 'L';
02410                 memcpy(temp + 2, str, len);
02411                 temp[len + 2] = ';';
02412                 temp[len + 3] = 0;
02413             }
02414 
02415             cl = bcl_resolve_class(cl, temp);
02416             gc_free(temp);
02417             opcode = ANEWARRAY;
02418             store_int16_un(pc + 1, class_get_id(cl));
02419         }
02420             break;
02421 
02422         case CHECKCAST_PRELINK:
02423             resolve_class(cl, index);
02424             opcode = CHECKCAST;
02425             store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02426             break;
02427 
02428         case INSTANCEOF_PRELINK:
02429             resolve_class(cl, index);
02430             opcode = INSTANCEOF;
02431             store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02432             break;
02433 
02434         case MULTIANEWARRAY_PRELINK:
02435         {
02436             uint8_t dimensions = *(pc + 3);
02437             cl = resolve_class(cl, index);
02438 
02439             if (class_get_dimensions(cl) < dimensions) {
02440                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
02441                         "MULTIANEWARRAY specifies an erroneous number of "
02442                         "dimensions");
02443             }
02444 
02445             opcode = MULTIANEWARRAY;
02446             store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02447         }
02448             break;
02449 
02450         case LDC_PRELINK:
02451             if (cp_get_tag(cp, index) == CONSTANT_Class) {
02452                 resolve_class(cl, index);
02453             }
02454 
02455             opcode = LDC_REF;
02456             break;
02457 
02458         case LDC_W_PRELINK:
02459             if (cp_get_tag(cp, index) == CONSTANT_Class) {
02460                 resolve_class(cl, index);
02461             }
02462 
02463             opcode = LDC_W_REF;
02464             store_int16_un(pc + 1, index);
02465             break;
02466 
02467         default:
02468             dbg_unreachable();
02469     }
02470 
02471     *pc = opcode;
02472     tm_unlock();
02473     return pc;
02474 } // bcl_link_opcode()
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:38:45 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
